jsonLdBlocks.description: Free fencing cost estimator. Get a city-level estimate by material, length, and gates. No email, no phone, no lead-selling.

mainEntity.name: How much does fence installation cost on average?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a typical 150-foot backyard residential fence, expect $2,500 to $9,000 installed. Wood is the cheapest at $2,250-$6,750, vinyl runs $3,750-$9,000, and chain link comes in lowest at $1,200-$3,750.

mainEntity.name: What is cheaper, wood or vinyl fencing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wood is cheaper upfront ($15-$45/ft vs $25-$60/ft for vinyl), but vinyl wins on lifetime cost. Over 25 years, wood needs staining every 3-5 years and replacement around year 20.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a permit to install a fence?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most cities require a permit for fences over 6 feet tall, fences in front yards, fences near sidewalks or property lines, and any fence in a flood zone or HOA-managed community. Permits typically cost $50-$400.

mainEntity.name: How long does fence installation take?

acceptedAnswer.text: A standard 150-foot residential fence takes 2-4 days for a 2-person crew. Wood and chain link install fastest. Vinyl and aluminum require more precise post setting.

mainEntity.name: Should I install a fence myself to save money?

acceptedAnswer.text: DIY fence installation can save 40-60% on labor. The trade-off requires post-hole digging, concrete setting, and accurate measurement. Most people find chain link and pre-fab wood panels DIY-friendly.
